ocr.text: The analysis of genomic data is essential for understanding genetic variation and functional genomics in various species. The
accompanying table presents a comprehensive overview of the genomic characteristics across different categories, highlighting
key metrics such as the "No. of reads (a)" and the "No. of annotated genes." These metrics provide insight into the sequencing
depth and the completeness of the genomic assembly, which are critical for subsequent analyses in comparative genomics and

evolutionary studies. 

The "No. of reads (a)" indicates the total number of sequencing reads obtained for each category, which
directly influences the resolution and reliability of the data. A higher number of reads typically correlates with increased
confidence in the identified variants and the reconstruction of the genome.
Category  No. of reads (a)  Length in Mb (%)  Predicted single-copy reads  Length in Mb  No. of contigs and singletons  Length in Mb  No. of SNPs  Nucleotides/SNP  No. of annotated genes
Genes
Repetitive sequences
Characterized repeats  7,121,948  2,818.1 (59.0%)
Additionally, the "No. of annotated genes" column reflects the extent of functional annotation achieved, offering a glimpse into
the biological relevance of the assembled sequences. This information is vital for researchers aiming to link genotype with
phenotype and for exploring the evolutionary trajectories of the species under study. Overall, the presented data underscore the
importance of robust genomic resources in advancing our understanding of genetic diversity and function.
